rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. VS. GURGEN ABRAHAMYANTS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiff'S Motion To Enforce Settlement (C.C.P. Section 664.6) Points And Authorities; And Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Monday, Apr-21-2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement (C.C.P. Section 664.6). Off calendar. The motion was filed on April 2, 2025, the 13th court day in advance of the hearing. CCP 1005(b) provides that "all moving and supporting papers shall be served and filed at least 16 court days before the hearing." For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND VINCENT DIGIACOMO JR. VS. UNION SQUARE BUSINESS IMPROVEMENT DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Monday, Apr-21-2025, Line 2. 1-OSC RE: DISMISSAL (PER ORDER). On February 21, 2025, the court granted the motion of defendants Union Square Business Improvement District; Union Square Foundation; Mydatt Services, Inc.; BBB-CA, Inc.; Marisa Rodriguez; and Mark Purdy for an order requiring plaintiff Raymond DiGiacomo, Jr. to furnish security in the amount of $50,000 pursuant to CCP 391.1. The court ordered the security to be posted within 30 days of entry of the order. On March 12, 2025, the court granted the motion of defendants the City and County of San Francisco and Carla Short for an order requiring DiGiacomo to furnish security in the amount of $50,000 by April 14, 2025. The court also issued an order to show cause to DiGiacomo to show proof that he has satisfied both security orders or to show cause why the matter should not be dismissed pursuant to CCP 391.4. No proof of security having been furnished, the court now dismisses this matter as to all of the defendants for whom security was ordered. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES CHIZINSKI ET AL VS. APHRM CORPORATION, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendants Sean Coletta, Shara Coletta, And S4 Development, Llcs Further Supplemental Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, Requests For Production, Set One; And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Monday, Apr-21-2025, Line 11. 1-PLAINTIFF JAMES CHIZINSKI'S, Motion To Compel Defendants Sean Coletta, Shara Coletta, And S4 Development, Llcs Further Supplemental Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, Requests For Production, Set One; And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff James Chizinski's motion to compel defendants Sean Coletta, Shara Coletta, and S4 Development (collectively "Defendants") to provide further supplemental responses to his Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Request for Production of Documents, Set One, and for sanctions, is granted as set forth below. ***THE FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER XUE VS. ROUND ONE ENTERTAINMENT INC
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY/LAW & MOTION CALENDAR for Monday, Apr-21-2025, Line 9. DEFENDANT ROUND ONE ENTERTAINMENT INC'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the complaint is sustained and overruled in part. (CCP 430.10(e). In reviewing the sufficiency of a complaint, the court treats as true allegations of fact, but not of contentions, deductions, or conclusions. (Rakestraw v. Cal. Physicians' Serv. (2000) 81 Cal.App.4th 39, 43.) The court exercises its discretion to consider plaintiff's late-filed opposition. ***THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO ALL PARTIES*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
